_comment: Z-Fish species table. Lures are referenced by id (see lures.json) under condition keys: warm, cold, lowLight, clear, ffs. 'water' buckets the species today. 'range' is a coarse, human-readable hint and is the hook for the NEXT phase, where real occurrence data (iNaturalist / GBIF) keyed to lat/lon replaces water-type bucketing and fixes the wrong-fish bug. 'icon' selects the SVG drawn in the app.

species.name: Largemouth Bass
species.range: Widespread across North America; lakes, ponds, slow rivers.
species.howTo: Ambush predator on cover — grass lines, docks, laydowns. Work edges; slow in cold, fast & reactive when warm.

species.name: Smallmouth Bass
species.range: Cooler, clearer lakes and rivers; northern U.S. and upland reservoirs.
species.howTo: Loves rock, current seams, and clean bottom. Goby and crawfish forage — keep baits near the floor.

species.name: Crappie
species.range: Reservoirs and lakes nationwide; brush and standing timber.
species.howTo: Suspends around brush, bridge pilings, and standing timber. Tiny profiles, vertical or slow swim.

species.name: Bluegill / Panfish
species.range: Ponds and lakes across North America; shallow, weedy bays.
species.howTo: Bedding bays and shaded banks in warm months. Downsize everything; subtle presentations win.

species.name: Channel Catfish
species.range: Rivers, lakes, and reservoirs throughout the U.S.
species.howTo: Bottom feeders cueing on scent — deep holes, current breaks, after dark. Fish slow on the floor.

species.name: Striped Bass
species.range: Large reservoirs and coastal rivers; landlocked and migratory stocks.
species.howTo: Roams open water chasing bait schools; explodes on surface at dawn. Match the baitfish size.

species.name: Walleye
species.range: Northern lakes and large rivers; cool, low-light waters.
species.howTo: Low-light bottom hugger over sand/gravel. Slow drag and swim presentations near structure.

species.name: Chain Pickerel
species.range: Eastern U.S. lakes and slow rivers; thick vegetation.
species.howTo: Aggressive ambusher in vegetation — cool months especially. Fast, flashy retrieves draw strikes.

species.name: Redfish
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; flats, marshes, oyster bars.
species.howTo: Tails on flats, holds on oyster bars and dock shade. Cover water, then slow down on a found fish.

species.name: Speckled Trout
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; grass flats and estuaries.
species.howTo: Grass flats and potholes; suspends over deeper edges when cold. Soft jerks and paddles on light jigs.

species.name: Flounder
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; sandy inlets and creek mouths.
species.howTo: Buried ambusher on sandy edges, inlets, and creek mouths. Drag baits slowly along the bottom.

species.name: Snook
species.range: Florida and warm Gulf waters; mangroves, docks, passes.
species.howTo: Cover-oriented around mangroves, docks, and lights at night. Reaction baits and natural soft plastics.

species.name: Black Drum
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; bars, pilings, and channels.
species.howTo: Roots along bars and pilings for crustaceans. Slow, scent-forward bottom presentations.

species.name: Sheepshead
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; pilings, jetties, reefs.
species.howTo: Structure nibbler on pilings, jetties, and reefs. Compact crustacean profiles fished tight to cover.

species.name: Spanish Mackerel
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; nearshore schools, warm months.
species.howTo: Fast schoolers smashing glass minnows near the surface. Speed and flash trigger reaction strikes.

species.name: Bluefish
species.range: Atlantic coast; surf, inlets, and nearshore bait blitzes.
species.howTo: Aggressive blitzes on bait balls. Burn a durable swimbait through the chaos.

species.name: Cobia
species.range: Atlantic and Gulf coasts; buoys, wrecks, surface structure.
species.howTo: Sight-fished on the surface near buoys, rays, and structure. Pitch a big profile ahead of the fish.
